item.title: Lucid Bots raises $20M to keep up with demand for its window-washing drones
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: Lucid Bots has seen demand accelerate over the last year for its window cleaning drones and power washing robots.

item.title: With $3.5B in fresh capital, Kleiner Perkins is going all in on AI
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The fundraise includes $1 billion for investing in early-stage startups, and $2.5 billion for late-stage growth businesses.

item.title: OpenAI’s Sora was the creepiest app on your phone — now it’s shutting down
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Though the underlying Sora 2 video- and audio-generation model is scarily impressive, there was not sustained interest in an AI-only social feed.

item.title: Kentucky woman rejects $26M offer to turn her farm into a data center
item.dc:creator: Graham Starr
item.description: A "major artificial intelligence company" reportedly offered a Kentucky family $26 million to build a data center on their farm.

item.title: Anthropic hands Claude Code more control, but keeps it on a leash
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic’s new auto mode for Claude Code lets AI execute tasks with fewer approvals, reflecting a broader shift toward more autonomous tools that balance speed with safety through built-in safeguards.

item.title: Spotify tests new tool to stop AI slop from being attributed to real artists
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The idea behind the new tool is to give artists more control over which tracks are associated with their name on Spotify.

item.title: Databricks bought two startups to underpin its new AI security product
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: With an overflowing war chest from its recent $5 billion raise, Databricks is buying startups and looking for more. It acquired Antimatter and SiftD.ai.

item.title: Arm is releasing the first in-house chip in its 35-year history
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: Arm is producing its own CPU for the first time. It developed the CPU with Meta, which is also the chip's first customer.

item.title: OpenAI’s plans to make ChatGPT more like Amazon aren’t going so well
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: OpenAI says it's moving away from Instant Checkout, which allowed users to buy items directly through the ChatGPT interface.

item.title: Google TV’s new Gemini features keep fans updated on sports teams and more
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Three Gemini-powered features are coming to your Google TV. This includes visual responses, deep dives, and sports briefs.

item.title: OpenAI adds open source tools to help developers build for teen safety
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Rather than working from scratch to figure out how to make AI safer for teens, developers can use these policies to fortify what they build.

item.title: Talat’s AI meeting notes stay on your machine, not in the cloud
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The subscription-free AI meeting notes app is a local-first twist on notetaking tools like Granola.

item.title: Doss raises $55M for AI inventory management that plugs into ERP
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Doss's AI-powered inventory management system integrates with existing ERP systems. The Series B round was co-led by Madrona and Premji Invest.

item.title: Meet the former Apple designer building a new AI interface at Hark
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The company said it would design models, hardware, and interfaces in tandem to deliver a "seamless end-to-end personal intelligence product."

item.title: Mirage raises $75M to continue building models for its AI video-editing app Captions
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Mirage, the maker of video-editing app Captions, has raised $75 million in growth financing from General Catalyst's Customer Value Fund (CVF).

item.title: Agile Robots becomes the latest robotics company to partner with Google DeepMind
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: Agile Robots will incorporate Google DeepMind's robotics foundation models into its bots while collecting data for the AI research lab.

item.title: Air Street becomes one of the largest solo VCs in Europe with $232M fund
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: London’s Air Street Capital has raised a large Fund III with eyes locked on backing early-stage European and North American AI companies.

item.title: Bernie Sanders’ AI ‘gotcha’ video flops, but the memes are great
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders thinks he's tricked Claude into revealing the AI industry's secrets, but he really just exposed how agreeable chatbots can become.

item.title: Vibe-coding startup Lovable is on the hunt for acquisitions
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Lovable's founder said the fast-growing vibe-coding startup is looking for startups and teams to join its company.

item.title: Apple sets June date for WWDC 2026, teasing ‘AI advancements’
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Apple will host its next Worldwide Developers Conference the week of June 8. The company is expected to announce major updates to Siri with advanced AI capabilities.
